name: publish_artifact
description: Publish a new artifact into the workspace. The workspace assigns a unique ID and routes the artifact to disk under workspace/{project_code}/{responsibility_code}/. Returns the new artifact ID. If this artifact supersedes existing artifacts (e.g. after resolving feedback), list their IDs in `supersedes`; those artifacts are retired from the live workspace but preserved on disk for audit.

type.description: Artifact type. Known types: narrative, architecture, requirements, functional-design, design-plan, tech-stack, code, test, feedback. New types may be introduced by convention.

author.description: Name of the agent publishing this artifact (e.g. 'narrative_author', 'architect', 'requirements_author'). Used by the workspace engine to route feedback and sequence agent work.

project_code.description: PROJECTCODE assigned by Architect (e.g. ETRD). Every artifact carries this, including project-wide ones.

responsibility_code.description: RESPONSIBILITYCODE assigned by Architect (e.g. AUTH). For project-wide artifacts (narrative, architecture, design-plan, tech-stack) use the PROJECTCODE value here as well.

requirement_ids.description: Requirement IDs (PROJECTCODE_RESPONSIBILITYCODE_REQUIREMENTCODE) this artifact satisfies or relates to. Omit for artifacts that predate or span requirements (narrative, architecture, design-plan, tech-stack).

content.description: Full text content of the artifact.

filename_hint.description: Suggested leaf filename (no path), e.g. 'requirements.md' or 'auth_service.py'. The workspace derives the directory from codenames; this influences only the leaf name.

supersedes.description: IDs of existing live artifacts this new artifact replaces. All listed artifacts are retired. Use when resolving feedback. Supports 1-to-1, 1-to-N (one old replaced by several new), and N-to-1 (several old merged into one new, by listing the same supersedes list on each new artifact).

reviewed_artifact_id.description: Required when type is 'feedback'. The ID of the artifact being reviewed.

verdict.description: Required when type is 'feedback'. Whether the reviewed artifact passed ('accepted') or requires rework ('rejected'). When 'rejected', the concerns list must be non-empty.

concerns.description: Required when type is 'feedback' and verdict is 'rejected'. Each entry is one specific issue found in the reviewed artifact. The valid 'kind' values are defined per critic agent; the shared base vocabulary is: ambiguity, contradiction, gap, compound, uncaptured_assumption, unmeasurable, missing_actor, requirement_uncovered, interface_mismatch, multiple_responsibilities.

kind.description: Category of the concern. Use a value from the critic agent's defined vocabulary; do not invent new kinds.

first_line.description: Line number of the first line of the problematic block in the artifact content.

last_line.description: Line number of the last line of the problematic block in the artifact content. Must be >= first_line.

excerpt.description: Verbatim text quoted from the artifact that the concern refers to.

description.description: The concern in plain language: what the problem is and why it matters.

metadata.description: Optional key-value pairs for additional context: iteration number, critic pass, etc. Values must be strings. Do not repeat 'author' here; use the top-level author field.
